title: Missing query parameter in RESTful API for the Libpod documentation
body:
### Issue Description

It is not mentioned in Query parameters for "Play a Kubernetes YAML file", that one can also give annotations. It seems though to be possible and that's Great :-)

It should be mentioned in the documentation so that it won't be mistakenly removed from implementation :smile: 

 [Play a Kubernetes YAML file](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/containers/operation/PlayKubeLibpod)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

 [Play a Kubernetes YAML file](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/containers/operation/PlayKubeLibpod)

1. one can see that annotations is not mentioned in the list of Query parameters


### Describe the results you received

One can though give annotations as a parameter in the api call, and it works just fine.`cat alpine-pod.yaml | curl  -H "Content-Type: 

```
$ cat alpine-pod.yaml | curl  -H "Content-Type: application/yaml" -vvv --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock --data-binary "@-" 'http://d/v4.0.0/libpod/play/kube?annotations=%7B%22some.value%22%3A%221%22%2C%22some.hash%22%3A%22911e0f%22%7D&network=pasta&replace=true'
* processing: http://d/v4.0.0/libpod/play/kube?annotations=%7B%22some.value%22%3A%221%22%2C%22some.hash%22%3A%22911e0f%22%7D&network=pasta&replace=true
*   Trying /run/podman/podman.sock:0...

```
And when inspecting container one can see:
```
               },
               "Annotations": {
                   <...>
                    "some.hash": "911e0f",
                    "some.value": "1"
               },

```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected that the annotations would be mentioned in the documentation also

### podman info output

```yaml
os: Linux
arch: arm64
buildahVersion: 1.32.0
podman version: 4.7.2
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
